verses.latin_vulgate: Omnia hæc tractavi in corde meo,  ut curiose intelligerem.  Sunt justi atque sapientes,  et opera eorum in manu Dei ;  et tamen nescit homo utrum amore an odio dignus sit.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: כִּ֣י אֶת־כׇּל־זֶ֞ה נָתַ֤תִּי אֶל־לִבִּי֙ וְלָב֣וּר אֶת־כׇּל־זֶ֔ה אֲשֶׁ֨ר הַצַּדִּיקִ֧ים וְהַחֲכָמִ֛ים וַעֲבָדֵיהֶ֖ם בְּיַ֣ד הָאֱלֹהִ֑ים גַּֽם־אַהֲבָ֣ה גַם־שִׂנְאָ֗ה אֵ֤ין יוֹדֵ֙עַ֙ הָֽאָדָ֔ם הַכֹּ֖ל לִפְנֵיהֶֽם׃
verses.king_james: For all this I considered in my heart even to declare all this, that the righteous, and the wise, and their works, are in the hand of God: no man knoweth either love or hatred by all that is before them.

verses.latin_vulgate: Sed omnia in futurum servantur incerta,  eo quod universa æque eveniant justo et impio,  bono et malo, mundo et immundo,  immolanti victimas et sacrificia contemnenti.  Sicut bonus, sic et peccator ;  ut perjurus, ita et ille qui verum dejerat.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: הַכֹּ֞ל כַּאֲשֶׁ֣ר לַכֹּ֗ל מִקְרֶ֨ה אֶחָ֜ד לַצַּדִּ֤יק וְלָרָשָׁע֙ לַטּוֹב֙ וְלַטָּה֣וֹר וְלַטָּמֵ֔א וְלַ֨זֹּבֵ֔חַ וְלַאֲשֶׁ֖ר אֵינֶ֣נּוּ זֹבֵ֑חַ כַּטּוֹב֙ כַּֽחֹטֶ֔א הַנִּשְׁבָּ֕ע כַּאֲשֶׁ֖ר שְׁבוּעָ֥ה יָרֵֽא׃
verses.king_james: All things come alike to all: there is one event to the righteous, and to the wicked; to the good and to the clean, and to the unclean; to him that sacrificeth, and to him that sacrificeth not: as is the good, so is the sinner; and he that sweareth, as he that feareth an oath.

verses.latin_vulgate: Hoc est pessimum inter omnia quæ sub sole fiunt :  quia eadem cunctis eveniunt.  Unde et corda filiorum hominum implentur malitia  et contemptu in vita sua,  et post hæc ad inferos deducentur.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: זֶ֣ה ׀ רָ֗ע בְּכֹ֤ל אֲשֶֽׁר־נַעֲשָׂה֙ תַּ֣חַת הַשֶּׁ֔מֶשׁ כִּֽי־מִקְרֶ֥ה אֶחָ֖ד לַכֹּ֑ל וְגַ֣ם לֵ֣ב בְּֽנֵי־הָ֠אָדָ֠ם מָלֵא־רָ֨ע וְהוֹלֵל֤וֹת בִּלְבָבָם֙ בְּחַיֵּיהֶ֔ם וְאַחֲרָ֖יו אֶל־הַמֵּתִֽים׃
verses.king_james: This is an evil among all things that are done under the sun, that there is one event unto all: yea, also the heart of the sons of men is full of evil, and madness is in their heart while they live, and after that they go to the dead.

verses.latin_vulgate: Nemo est qui semper vivat, et qui hujus rei habeat fiduciam ;  melior est canis vivus leone mortuo.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: כִּי־מִי֙ אֲשֶׁ֣ר (יבחר) [יְחֻבַּ֔ר] אֶ֥ל כׇּל־הַחַיִּ֖ים יֵ֣שׁ בִּטָּח֑וֹן כִּֽי־לְכֶ֤לֶב חַי֙ ה֣וּא ט֔וֹב מִן־הָאַרְיֵ֖ה הַמֵּֽת׃
verses.king_james: For to him that is joined to all the living there is hope: for a living dog is better than a dead lion.

verses.latin_vulgate: Viventes enim sciunt se esse morituros ;  mortui vero nihil noverunt amplius,  nec habent ultra mercedem,  quia oblivioni tradita est memoria eorum.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: כִּ֧י הַֽחַיִּ֛ים יוֹדְעִ֖ים שֶׁיָּמֻ֑תוּ וְהַמֵּתִ֞ים אֵינָ֧ם יוֹדְעִ֣ים מְא֗וּמָה וְאֵֽין־ע֤וֹד לָהֶם֙ שָׂכָ֔ר כִּ֥י נִשְׁכַּ֖ח זִכְרָֽם׃
verses.king_james: For the living know that they shall die: but the dead know not any thing, neither have they any more a reward; for the memory of them is forgotten.

verses.latin_vulgate: Amor quoque, et odium, et invidiæ simul perierunt ;  nec habent partem in hoc sæculo,  et in opere quod sub sole geritur.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: גַּ֣ם אַהֲבָתָ֧ם גַּם־שִׂנְאָתָ֛ם גַּם־קִנְאָתָ֖ם כְּבָ֣ר אָבָ֑דָה וְחֵ֨לֶק אֵין־לָהֶ֥ם עוֹד֙ לְעוֹלָ֔ם בְּכֹ֥ל אֲשֶֽׁר־נַעֲשָׂ֖ה תַּ֥חַת הַשָּֽׁמֶשׁ׃
verses.king_james: Also their love, and their hatred, and their envy, is now perished; neither have they any more a portion for ever in any thing that is done under the sun.

verses.latin_vulgate: Vade ergo, et comede in lætitia panem tuum,  et bibe cum gaudio vinum tuum,  quia Deo placent opera tua.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: לֵ֣ךְ אֱכֹ֤ל בְּשִׂמְחָה֙ לַחְמֶ֔ךָ וּֽשְׁתֵ֥ה בְלֶב־ט֖וֹב יֵינֶ֑ךָ כִּ֣י כְבָ֔ר רָצָ֥ה הָאֱלֹהִ֖ים אֶֽת־מַעֲשֶֽׂיךָ׃
verses.king_james: Go thy way, eat thy bread with joy, and drink thy wine with a merry heart; for God now accepteth thy works.

verses.latin_vulgate: Omni tempore sint vestimenta tua candida,  et oleum de capite tuo non deficiat.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: בְּכׇל־עֵ֕ת יִהְי֥וּ בְגָדֶ֖יךָ לְבָנִ֑ים וְשֶׁ֖מֶן עַל־רֹאשְׁךָ֥ אַל־יֶחְסָֽר׃
verses.king_james: Let thy garments be always white; and let thy head lack no ointment.

verses.latin_vulgate: Perfruere vita cum uxore quam diligis,  cunctis diebus vitæ instabilitatis tuæ,  qui dati sunt tibi sub sole omni tempore vanitatis tuæ :  hæc est enim pars in vita  et in labore tuo quo laboras sub sole.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: רְאֵ֨ה חַיִּ֜ים עִם־אִשָּׁ֣ה אֲשֶׁר־אָהַ֗בְתָּ כׇּל־יְמֵי֙ חַיֵּ֣י הֶבְלֶ֔ךָ אֲשֶׁ֤ר נָֽתַן־לְךָ֙ תַּ֣חַת הַשֶּׁ֔מֶשׁ כֹּ֖ל יְמֵ֣י הֶבְלֶ֑ךָ כִּ֣י ה֤וּא חֶלְקְךָ֙ בַּֽחַיִּ֔ים וּבַעֲמָ֣לְךָ֔ אֲשֶׁר־אַתָּ֥ה עָמֵ֖ל תַּ֥חַת הַשָּֽׁמֶשׁ׃
verses.king_james: Live joyfully with the wife whom thou lovest all the days of the life of thy vanity, which he hath given thee under the sun, all the days of thy vanity: for that is thy portion in this life, and in thy labour which thou takest under the sun.

verses.latin_vulgate: Quodcumque facere potest manus tua,  instanter operare,  quia nec opus, nec ratio, nec sapientia, nec scientia  erunt apud inferos, quo tu properas.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: כֹּ֠ל אֲשֶׁ֨ר תִּמְצָ֧א יָֽדְךָ֛ לַעֲשׂ֥וֹת בְּכֹחֲךָ֖ עֲשֵׂ֑ה כִּי֩ אֵ֨ין מַעֲשֶׂ֤ה וְחֶשְׁבּוֹן֙ וְדַ֣עַת וְחׇכְמָ֔ה בִּשְׁא֕וֹל אֲשֶׁ֥ר אַתָּ֖ה הֹלֵ֥ךְ שָֽׁמָּה׃
verses.king_james: Whatsoever thy hand findeth to do, do it with thy might; for there is no work, nor device, nor knowledge, nor wisdom, in the grave, whither thou goest.

verses.latin_vulgate: Verti me ad aliud, et vidi sub sole  nec velocium esse cursum,  nec fortium bellum,  nec sapientium panem,  nec doctorum divitias,  nec artificum gratiam ;  sed tempus casumque in omnibus.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: שַׁ֜בְתִּי וְרָאֹ֣ה תַֽחַת־הַשֶּׁ֗מֶשׁ כִּ֣י לֹא֩ לַקַּלִּ֨ים הַמֵּר֜וֹץ וְלֹ֧א לַגִּבּוֹרִ֣ים הַמִּלְחָמָ֗ה וְ֠גַ֠ם לֹ֣א לַחֲכָמִ֥ים לֶ֙חֶם֙ וְגַ֨ם לֹ֤א לַנְּבֹנִים֙ עֹ֔שֶׁר וְגַ֛ם לֹ֥א לַיֹּדְעִ֖ים חֵ֑ן כִּי־עֵ֥ת וָפֶ֖גַע יִקְרֶ֥ה אֶת־כֻּלָּֽם׃
verses.king_james: I returned, and saw under the sun, that the race is not to the swift, nor the battle to the strong, neither yet bread to the wise, nor yet riches to men of understanding, nor yet favour to men of skill; but time and chance happeneth to them all.

verses.latin_vulgate: Nescit homo finem suum ;  sed sicut pisces capiuntur hamo,  et sicut aves laqueo comprehenduntur,  sic capiuntur homines in tempore malo,  cum eis extemplo supervenerit.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: כִּ֡י גַּם֩ לֹֽא־יֵדַ֨ע הָאָדָ֜ם אֶת־עִתּ֗וֹ כַּדָּגִים֙ שֶׁנֶּֽאֱחָזִים֙ בִּמְצוֹדָ֣ה רָעָ֔ה וְכַ֨צִּפֳּרִ֔ים הָאֲחֻז֖וֹת בַּפָּ֑ח כָּהֵ֗ם יֽוּקָשִׁים֙ בְּנֵ֣י הָֽאָדָ֔ם לְעֵ֣ת רָעָ֔ה כְּשֶׁתִּפּ֥וֹל עֲלֵיהֶ֖ם פִּתְאֹֽם׃
verses.king_james: For man also knoweth not his time: as the fishes that are taken in an evil net, and as the birds that are caught in the snare; so are the sons of men snared in an evil time, when it falleth suddenly upon them.

verses.latin_vulgate: Hanc quoque sub sole vidi sapientiam,  et probavi maximam :
verses.hebrew_masoretic: גַּם־זֹ֛ה רָאִ֥יתִי חׇכְמָ֖ה תַּ֣חַת הַשָּׁ֑מֶשׁ וּגְדוֹלָ֥ה הִ֖יא אֵלָֽי׃
verses.king_james: This wisdom have I seen also under the sun, and it seemed great unto me:

verses.latin_vulgate: civitas parva, et pauci in ea viri ;  venit contra eam rex magnus, et vallavit eam,  exstruxitque munitiones per gyrum, et perfecta est obsidio.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: עִ֣יר קְטַנָּ֔ה וַאֲנָשִׁ֥ים בָּ֖הּ מְעָ֑ט וּבָֽא־אֵלֶ֜יהָ מֶ֤לֶךְ גָּדוֹל֙ וְסָבַ֣ב אֹתָ֔הּ וּבָנָ֥ה עָלֶ֖יהָ מְצוֹדִ֥ים גְּדֹלִֽים׃
verses.king_james: There was a little city, and few men within it; and there came a great king against it, and besieged it, and built great bulwarks against it:

verses.latin_vulgate: Inventusque est in ea vir pauper et sapiens,  et liberavit urbem per sapientiam suam ;  et nullus deinceps recordatus est hominis illius pauperis.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וּמָ֣צָא בָ֗הּ אִ֤ישׁ מִסְכֵּן֙ חָכָ֔ם וּמִלַּט־ה֥וּא אֶת־הָעִ֖יר בְּחׇכְמָת֑וֹ וְאָדָם֙ לֹ֣א זָכַ֔ר אֶת־הָאִ֥ישׁ הַמִּסְכֵּ֖ן הַהֽוּא׃
verses.king_james: Now there was found in it a poor wise man, and he by his wisdom delivered the city; yet no man remembered that same poor man.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et dicebam ego meliorem esse sapientiam fortitudine.  Quomodo ergo sapientia pauperis contempta est,  et verba ejus non sunt audita ?
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וְאָמַ֣רְתִּי אָ֔נִי טוֹבָ֥ה חׇכְמָ֖ה מִגְּבוּרָ֑ה וְחׇכְמַ֤ת הַמִּסְכֵּן֙ בְּזוּיָ֔ה וּדְבָרָ֖יו אֵינָ֥ם נִשְׁמָעִֽים׃
verses.king_james: Then said I, Wisdom is better than strength: nevertheless the poor man’s wisdom is despised, and his words are not heard.

verses.latin_vulgate: Verba sapientium audiuntur in silentio,  plus quam clamor principis inter stultos.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: דִּבְרֵ֣י חֲכָמִ֔ים בְּנַ֖חַת נִשְׁמָעִ֑ים מִזַּעֲקַ֥ת מוֹשֵׁ֖ל בַּכְּסִילִֽים׃
verses.king_james: The words of wise men are heard in quiet more than the cry of him that ruleth among fools.

verses.latin_vulgate: Melior est sapientia quam arma bellica ;  et qui in uno peccaverit, multa bona perdet.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: טוֹבָ֥ה חׇכְמָ֖ה מִכְּלֵ֣י קְרָ֑ב וְחוֹטֶ֣א אֶחָ֔ד יְאַבֵּ֖ד טוֹבָ֥ה הַרְבֵּֽה׃
verses.king_james: Wisdom is better than weapons of war: but one sinner destroyeth much good.
